name: Banana Cake with Caramel Cream Cheese Frostin
recipeInstructions:
Caramel Glaze:
Packed brown sugar - 1/4 cup
Sweetened condensed milk - 1/4 cup
Unsalted butter, softened - 2 tablespoons
Confectioners' sugar - 1 cup
Pure vanilla extract - 1 teaspoon
Frosting:
Cream cheese, softened - 3 ounces
Unsalted butter, softened - 6 tablespoons
Sour cream - 2 tablespoons
Pure maple syrup - 2 tablespoons
Pure vanilla extract - 1 teaspoon
Confectioners' sugar - 3 to 3 1/2 cups
Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Grease a 13- by 9-inch baking pan. Sift flour, baking soda, and salt together; set aside.
Beat butter and granulated sugar in electric mixer on high until light, about 2 minutes. Add eggs one at a time, mixing well after each addition. Stop the mixer and add bananas, sour cream, and vanilla; mix on low speed. Fold dry ingredients and nuts with rubber spatula.
Transfer batter to prepared pan. Bake until toothpick inserted in center comes out clean, about 35 minutes. Cool completely on wire rack.
For caramel glaze, combine brown sugar and condensed milk in saucepan. Cook over medium heat, stirring often, until mixture begins to bubble. Reduce heat to low and add butter; cook and stir until melted. Remove from heat and stir in confectioners' sugar and vanilla. Spread over cooled cake.
For frosting, beat cream cheese and butter until smooth. Stir in sour cream, maple, and vanilla. Add enough confectioners' sugar to make a smooth, thick frosting. Carefully spread over caramel glaze.
